articles.headline: Retail inflation jumps to 14-month high of 6.21% in October
articles.summary: India's retail inflation surged to 6.21% in October, breaching the RBI's 6% upper tolerance limit for the first time in over a year. The spike was primarily driven by a sharp rise in food prices, with food inflation hitting 10.87%. This makes an interest rate cut in the upcoming December policy meeting highly unlikely as the central bank prioritizes price stability.

articles.headline: Industrial production growth recovers to 3.1% in September
articles.summary: India’s industrial output growth rebounded to 3.1% in September 2024 after a surprise contraction in August. The recovery was led by a 3.9% growth in the manufacturing sector and 0.5% in electricity. Mining output remained flat. Cumulative growth for the first half of the fiscal year (April-September) stands at 4.0%, reflecting a steady but cautious expansion in the industrial sector.

articles.headline: Swiggy shares debut with nearly 17% premium on stock exchanges
articles.summary: Food delivery giant Swiggy made a successful market debut as its shares listed at Rs 420 on the NSE, a 7.7% premium over its IPO price of Rs 390. The stock continued to gain throughout the day, closing at Rs 455.95, up 16.9% from the issue price. The Rs 11,327-crore IPO had seen strong institutional interest despite high market volatility and concerns over profitability.

articles.headline: Supreme Court stays demolition of properties linked to accused persons
articles.summary: The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment against 'bulldozer justice,' ruling that the executive cannot demolish properties of individuals just because they are accused of a crime. The court held that such actions bypass legal processes and violate the separation of powers. It mandated a 15-day notice period and proper documentation before any demolition activity.

articles.headline: Tata Power reports 7% rise in Q2 net profit
articles.summary: Tata Power announced a 7.4% year-on-year increase in its consolidated net profit to Rs 1,093 crore for the September quarter. The company’s revenue stood at Rs 15,648 crore. The profit growth was supported by strong performance in the solar and transmission segments. The company continues to transition towards renewable energy with a significant pipeline of green projects.

articles.headline: Reliance Industries to invest Rs 65,000 crore in Andhra Pradesh
articles.summary: Reliance Industries signed a pact with the Andhra Pradesh government to invest Rs 65,000 crore for setting up 500 compressed biogas plants over the next five years. This project is expected to generate 2.5 lakh direct and indirect jobs. The initiative focuses on sustainable energy and leveraging agricultural waste, marking a major expansion of RIL's green energy footprint.

articles.headline: Government to sell 2.5% stake in NTPC via offer for sale
articles.summary: The Indian government has launched an Offer for Sale (OFS) to divest a 2.5% stake in NTPC, the country's largest power producer. The floor price for the sale has been set at Rs 410 per share. The divestment aims to raise roughly Rs 10,000 crore, helping the government reach its annual disinvestment target while maintaining a majority stake in the power major.

articles.headline: BCCI announces squad for upcoming T20I series in South Africa
articles.summary: The BCCI has named a 16-member squad for the four-match T20I series against South Africa starting later this month. Suryakumar Yadav will lead the side, which features several fresh faces like Ramandeep Singh and Vijaykumar Vyshak. Senior players remain rested in preparation for the upcoming Test series, giving younger players an opportunity to showcase talent in overseas conditions.

articles.headline: Yamuna River nearly dry as Delhi faces severe water shortage
articles.summary: Large parts of the Yamuna near the Wazirabad barrage have dried up, with water levels dropping five feet below normal. The Wazirabad and Chandrawal treatment plants are running below capacity, leading to water supply cuts in areas including Rajouri Garden, Rajinder Nagar, and Patel Nagar. Excavators are being used to channel remaining water to treatment plants.

articles.headline: Congress summons Karnataka CM and Deputy CM to Delhi
articles.summary: The Congress leadership has summoned Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah and DK Shivakumar to Delhi to resolve a protracted leadership tussle. While the party calls it a routine meet for upcoming elections, sources suggest a power-sharing formula may involve Siddaramaiah moving to the Rajya Sabha in exchange for a cabinet position for his son, Yathindra.

articles.headline: Three rebel AIADMK legislators resign, likely to join TVK
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu Speaker JCD Prabhakar accepted the resignations of AIADMK MLAs Maragatham Kumaravel, P Sathyabama, and S Jayakumar. The trio had previously defied a party whip to support the TVK government. While they hint at joining the ruling TVK, five other rebel legislators have returned to the Edappadi K Palaniswami-led AIADMK camp.

articles.headline: Assam tables UCC bill mandating registration of live-in relationships
articles.summary: The Assam government introduced the Uniform Civil Code Bill, 2026, making the registration of live-in relationships and all marriages/divorces mandatory. The bill bans polygamy but excludes Scheduled Tribes from its ambit. Failure to register a live-in relationship can lead to six months in prison or a ₹25,000 fine. It also sets the minimum marriage age at 18 for women and 21 for men.

articles.headline: Delhi High Court to hear challenge against Gymkhana Club eviction
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court will hear challenges to a central government order directing the Delhi Gymkhana Club to vacate its 27.3-acre premises within 15 days. Separate suits filed by a club member and the staff welfare association are scheduled for a hearing before Justice Avneesh Jhingan on Tuesday.

articles.headline: CBSE admits error in uploading student’s Physics answer sheet
articles.summary: CBSE acknowledged a mix-up where an incorrect scanned answer sheet was uploaded against Delhi student Vedant Shrivastava's roll number. Shrivastava flagged the discrepancy online after noticing the script did not match his handwriting. The board has sent the correct copy and confirmed that his Class 12 marks will be updated accordingly.

articles.headline: Over 300 tourists rescued after Gulmarg Gondola technical snag
articles.summary: More than 300 tourists were stranded for seven hours mid-air in 65 cabins of the Gulmarg Gondola cable car following a major technical failure on Monday. All holidayers were eventually rescued safely. Separately, police in Agra arrested two people after a 16-year-old boy fell to his death from a 45-foot-high zipline at an amusement park.

articles.headline: CBI takes over probe into MP actor Twisha Sharma death
articles.summary: The CBI has taken over the investigation into the death of 32-year-old actor-model Twisha Sharma in Madhya Pradesh. The agency re-registered the case against Sharma’s husband and mother-in-law following allegations of institutional bias and procedural lapses in the initial state police probe. The Supreme Court had previously noted concerns regarding the husband's legal background.

articles.headline: Enhanced Games debut sees world records in drug-friendly events
articles.summary: Greek swimmer Kristian Gkolomeev claimed a $1 million bonus at the debut of the drug-friendly Enhanced Games in Las Vegas by swimming the 50m freestyle in 20.81 seconds. The time is 0.07 seconds faster than the official world record but will not be recognized by WADA. American sprinter Fred Kerley won the 100m event in 9.97 seconds under the same unregulated conditions.

articles.headline: El Nino likely to suppress Indian monsoon rainfall in 2026
articles.summary: Meteorological data suggests 2026 will be an El Nino year, likely causing disappointing monsoon rains. Analysis shows El Nino conditions correlate with a reduced temperature gradient between the Indian landmass and the Indian Ocean, which is a key driver for monsoon winds. This gradient, typically favorable from June to September, is weakened by cyclical Pacific warming.

articles.headline: Trump urges Arab nations and Pakistan to recognize Israel
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has urged Pakistan and several West Asian nations, including Saudi Arabia and Qatar, to sign the Abraham Accords and recognize Israel. Trump stated that formal recognition is mandatory for a broader diplomatic effort to end the US-Iran conflict. The remarks follow weekend talks with regional leaders regarding a potential peace settlement.

articles.headline: SC refuses to clarify verdict on euthanizing dangerous stray dogs
articles.summary: The Supreme Court declined to issue clarifications on its recent judgment allowing authorities to euthanize rabid, incurably ill, or demonstrably dangerous stray dogs. Responding to an NGO's concerns about indiscriminate culling, the bench maintained that public safety and the protection of human life cannot be compromised by an overly broad reading of animal welfare rules.

articles.headline: Fuel excise duty cuts to cost exchequer ₹1 lakh crore: FM
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated that the government's decision to cut excise duty on petrol and diesel to shield consumers from rising crude prices will cost over ₹1 lakh crore this financial year. She defended recent retail price hikes as market-driven revisions by oil marketing companies responding to global volatility caused by the West Asia war.

articles.headline: CBI to name three teachers in NEET-UG paper leak charge sheet
articles.summary: The CBI is set to file its first charge sheet in the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak case, naming teachers PV Kulkarni, Manisha Mandhare, and Manisha Havaldar as key figures. The probe found they accessed chemistry, biology, and physics questions days before the exam. No NTA officials have been named yet, but the agency says all aspects of the investigation remain open.

articles.headline: Kejriwal to participate in CBI appeal against excise case discharge
articles.summary: Former Delhi CM Arvind Kejriwal, Manish Sisodia, and Durgesh Pathak have decided to participate in the CBI's appeal challenging a trial court order that discharged them in the Delhi excise policy case. The matter is currently being heard by Justice Manoj Jain in the Delhi High Court.

articles.headline: Pope Leo XIV calls for 'disarming' of AI in first manifesto
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV issued his first encyclical, 'Magnifica Humanitas', calling for the international regulation of artificial intelligence. He warned that a race for algorithmic dominance could lead to 'new forms of slavery' and mass surveillance. The Pope urged for the 'disarming' of AI to prevent lethal autonomous warfare, supported by AI experts from Anthropic.

articles.headline: Cine employees union moves against actor Ranveer Singh
articles.summary: The Federation of Western India Cine Employees (FWICE) announced that its 400,000 members will not work with actor Ranveer Singh following his last-minute exit from the film 'Don 3'. Producers Farhan Akhtar and Ritesh Sidhwani filed a complaint alleging that over ₹45 crore had already been spent on pre-production. Singh stated he holds respect for the project team.

articles.headline: Eight dead after SUV plunges into Raigad gorge in Maharashtra
articles.summary: An SUV carrying eight people plunged 1,200 feet into a gorge in Maharashtra's Raigad district early Monday. The victims were returning from Karade beach in Dapoli when the accident occurred near Mahabaleshwar. All occupants died in the crash. Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis expressed grief and offered tributes to the deceased.

articles.headline: Heat dome causes record May temperatures in UK and France
articles.summary: Western Europe is experiencing a record-breaking heatwave caused by a 'heat dome' of warm air from North Africa. The UK recorded its hottest May day ever at 34.8°C. Forecasters warned of prolonged extreme heat across the continent throughout the week, with temperatures in Spain expected to peak at 38°C and Italy imposing outdoor work restrictions.

articles.headline: IPL 2026 playoffs begin with RCB vs GT in Qualifier 1
articles.summary: The IPL 2026 playoffs kick off in Dharamshala with Royal Challengers Bengaluru facing Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 1. The winner gains direct entry to the final. The Eliminator between Sunrisers Hyderabad and Rajasthan Royals will follow in Chandigarh. This season has seen a record 61 team totals of 200 or more, redefining T20 batting expectations.

articles.headline: Political storm over ₹18,000-crore Bidadi satellite township project
articles.summary: The proposed Greater Bengaluru Integrated Township near Bidadi has sparked a political clash in Karnataka. The JD(S) and farmer groups are protesting the acquisition of 7,481 acres of agricultural land, alleging it benefits real-estate interests. Deputy CM DK Shivakumar defended the project as necessary for planned urban growth, offering compensation packages to landowners.

articles.headline: Karnataka government slams Centre over 'fuel loot' and price hikes
articles.summary: The ruling Congress in Karnataka accused the Modi government of 'fuel loot' after the fourth petrol price hike in two weeks. CM Siddaramaiah and Randeep Surjewala questioned why prices are rising while global crude falls. Meanwhile, BJP leader R Ashoka hit back, blaming high state VAT for the ₹107-plus petrol price in Bengaluru compared to other states.

articles.headline: Karnataka to review Gruha Lakshmi beneficiaries for ineligible recipients
articles.summary: Deputy CM DK Shivakumar announced a review of the state's guarantee schemes, including Gruha Lakshmi, citing reports that ₹100 crore is going to ineligible recipients. Beneficiaries may be asked to resubmit documents to ensure funds reach the intended targets. The government has allocated over ₹51,000 crore for five key welfare schemes in 2026-27.

articles.headline: Bengaluru hospital staffer booked for recording woman in restroom
articles.summary: A 26-year-old housekeeping employee at Apollo Hospital on Sarjapur Road was booked for allegedly attempting to record a pregnant woman in a restroom. Police seized the suspect's mobile phone and sent it for forensic analysis to recover any deleted material. The hospital administration has removed the staffer from duty and is cooperating with the investigation.

articles.headline: Death toll reaches 11 in Uttara Kannada river drowning tragedy
articles.summary: The death toll from the Shirali village river tragedy rose to 11 after police recovered another body on Monday. A group of mussel collectors was swept away by strong currents in the Tatte Hakkalu river on Sunday. Three survivors are currently recovering in the hospital. The state government announced ₹5 lakh compensation for each of the deceased's families.

articles.headline: Tension in Bhatkal over demolition of religious structure; town calm
articles.summary: Prohibitory orders were imposed in Bhatkal, Karnataka, after tensions flared following the alleged demolition of a religious structure near NH66. While five cases were registered regarding the clash and social media instigation, authorities stated the situation is now under control. The structure was reportedly linked to the Marikamba temple and stood on government land.

articles.headline: Karnataka launches centralized command centre for 108 ambulance services
articles.summary: CM Siddaramaiah inaugurated a state-of-the-art Centralised Command and Control Centre in Bengaluru to manage emergency ambulance services across Karnataka. The fully government-owned system replaces the previous PPP model, aiming to improve response times and accountability. The project was originally announced in the 2025-26 state budget.

articles.headline: Hotels in Karnataka warn of price hikes following wage revision
articles.summary: The Karnataka State Hotels Association warned that a 60% increase in minimum wages for workers will lead to higher food prices. Association president GK Shetty said popular items like idli and masala dosa could see steep price jumps. The hike, combined with rising commercial LPG and grocery costs, is expected to severely impact small and family-run restaurants.

articles.headline: IMD issues red alert for heat wave in Telangana districts
articles.summary: The IMD issued a red alert for severe heat wave conditions in isolated pockets of Telangana, including Mulugu, Khammam, and Bhadradri Kothagudem. Residents were warned of high temperatures on Monday and Tuesday. The weather warning applies to several districts as the state grapples with intense summer heat.

articles.headline: Twelve sentenced to life in 2018 Madhu tribal lynching case
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court sentenced 12 individuals to life imprisonment for the 2018 lynching of Madhu, a tribal youth in Attappadi. The court modified a previous lower court ruling, describing the incident as a humiliating mob assault. Madhu was killed after being falsely accused of stealing groceries. The court noted the brutality of the public humiliation involved.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu CM announces full crop loan waiver for farmers
articles.summary: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay announced a full waiver of cooperative bank crop loans up to ₹50,000 for marginal farmers. The move will benefit 1.42 million farmers and cost the state exchequer ₹2,044 crore. Large farmers will receive a waiver of up to ₹5,000. The decision fulfills one of the key election promises made by the CM.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh to form task force to clean Godavari River
articles.summary: Deputy CM Pawan Kalyan announced a special task force to curb pollution in the Godavari River ahead of the spiritual Godavari Pushkarams. A ₹100 crore allocation was made for pollution control across six districts. The government also plans a year-long ban on plastic along the riverbanks and field-level inspections to identify industrial waste discharge points.

articles.headline: Higher education institutes seek autonomy in proposed VBSA Bill
articles.summary: Top institutions including BHU and IIT Dhanbad urged a parliamentary panel to safeguard institutional autonomy in the proposed Viksit Bharat Shiksha Adhishthan (VBSA) Bill. The bill aims to replace UGC and AICTE with a unified regulator. Institutions warned that current provisions could subordinate the regulator to the executive and trigger friction between Centre and states.

articles.headline: BJD MP Debashish Samantaray resigns, set to join BJP
articles.summary: In a blow to the Biju Janata Dal, Rajya Sabha MP Debashish Samantaray resigned from the party and his seat, alleging he was 'systematically belittled'. Samantaray, a long-time aide of Naveen Patnaik, is expected to join the BJP. His exit follows two other BJD Rajya Sabha members who joined the BJP earlier this year.

articles.headline: Nadda reviews Ebola preparedness as cases rise in Africa
articles.summary: Union Health Minister JP Nadda chaired a high-level meeting to review India's preparedness against the Ebola virus following outbreaks in Congo, Uganda, and South Sudan. He ordered mandatory screening of travelers from affected regions at all entry points. The DGCA has issued SOPs requiring self-declaration forms for international passengers arriving from DRC and Uganda.

articles.headline: Amit Shah to review security and unveil smart border projects
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah will visit border areas in Rajasthan and Gujarat to review security and unveil 'smart border' projects. His itinerary includes visits to Bikaner's Sanchu post and the strategically sensitive Harami Nala area in Kutch. Shah will meet with BSF officers and state officials to discuss strategies for addressing future western border threats.

articles.headline: UP gram pradhans to continue as administrators as tenure ends
articles.summary: As the tenure of gram panchayats ends on May 26, the Uttar Pradesh government has appointed sitting gram pradhans as administrators to maintain routine administrative work. This move, a first in the state's history, aims to avoid a vacuum before fresh elections. Administrators are restricted from making major policy decisions during their six-month maximum term.

articles.headline: India secures oil and fertilizer supplies despite West Asia crisis
articles.summary: Government officials informed a parliamentary panel that India has made adequate arrangements to procure oil and fertilizers from the US, Russia, and Africa to avoid shortfalls. The panel was told that 12 of the 36 ships currently stuck in the Strait of Hormuz are Indian. Most Indian sailors are safe and willing to continue working despite insurance premium hikes.

articles.headline: SC rules husband’s kin not liable in dowry cases without proof
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that husband's relatives cannot be held criminally liable in matrimonial disputes unless there is evidence of active complicity. Setting aside a case from Guna, MP, the bench stated that mere allegations of supporting the husband or advising the wife to 'adjust' do not constitute harassment or cruelty under criminal law.

articles.headline: Netanyahu admits limited influence over Trump’s Iran policy
articles.summary: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu reportedly told confidants that Israel has little ability to influence President Donald Trump’s decision-making regarding Iran negotiations. Netanyahu expressed concerns over a potential memorandum of understanding being discussed, as Israel remains largely excluded from talks to end the three-month-old regional conflict.

articles.headline: Xi Jinping meets Pakistan PM to discuss West Asia peace
articles.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping met with Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif in Beijing to discuss regional stability and efforts to end the Iran war. Xi praised Pakistan's role as a mediator between the US and Iran. Sharif was accompanied by Army Chief Asim Munir, who has emerged as a key negotiator in facilitating direct communications between Washington and Tehran.

articles.headline: Russia threatens strikes on Kyiv 'decision centres', warns foreigners
articles.summary: Russia announced plans to launch systematic strikes on military-industrial facilities and decision-making centers in Kyiv using hypersonic Oreshnik missiles. The Kremlin urged foreign diplomats and citizens to leave the city immediately, calling the strikes retaliation for a Ukrainian attack in Lugansk. Ukraine has dismissed the threats as psychological blackmail.

articles.headline: Zepto prepares for $1 billion IPO with June filing
articles.summary: Rapid-commerce platform Zepto is set to publicly file for an initial public offering (IPO) that could raise up to $1 billion. The 10-minute delivery company, valued at $7 billion, targets a launch in July. The offering is expected to include both fresh shares and secondary sales by existing investors to fund the company's planned expansion.

articles.headline: India Post and BSNL record strong revenue growth in FY26
articles.summary: India Post reported a revenue of ₹15,373 crore in 2025-26, driven by a 70% growth in its parcel and logistics business. Simultaneously, BSNL's revenue rose to ₹25,000 crore, a 25% increase over two years. The gains are attributed to technological upgrades, including indigenous 4G deployment and digital payment systems for postal services.

articles.headline: Indian refiners pivot to Latin America for oil amid Hormuz crisis
articles.summary: Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz led Indian refiners to significantly increase crude oil imports from Venezuela, Brazil, Angola, and Nigeria. While Russia remains the top supplier, the share of OPEC countries in India's oil imports rose to 45.2% in April. The pivot follows shipping restrictions caused by the conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran.

articles.headline: India plans ₹12.3 lakh crore investment in AI data centres
articles.summary: India's AI push is materializing in a massive digital warehouse boom, with 208 data centre projects worth ₹12.3 lakh crore in the pipeline. Maharashtra and Uttar Pradesh are leading the development. While the boom aims to add 20GW of capacity by 2030, experts suggest the capital-intensive sector may create fewer jobs than traditional IT services.

articles.headline: Iga Swiatek cruises to victory in French Open first round
articles.summary: Four-time French Open champion Iga Swiatek defeated Australian wildcard Emerson Jones 6-1, 6-2 in just 60 minutes to open her Roland Garros campaign. Swiatek, who has revamped her clay-court game under coach Francisco Roig, showed dominant form with 17 winners. The victory signals her strong intent to defend her title following a successful run in Rome.

articles.headline: Nishesh Basavareddy stuns World No. 9 Taylor Fritz at Roland Garros
articles.summary: In a major upset at the French Open, Indian-origin American player Nishesh Basavareddy defeated seventh-seeded Taylor Fritz in the first round. The 21-year-old Basavareddy won 7-6(5), 7-6(5), 6-7(9), 6-1 in a match lasting nearly three hours. He is the first American man to beat a top-10 opponent at Roland Garros since 2000.

articles.headline: FY27 fertilizer subsidy may top ₹3 lakh cr on price surge
articles.summary: India's fertilizer subsidy could exceed ₹3 lakh crore in FY27, nearly double the Budget Estimate of ₹1.71 lakh crore, driven by soaring global prices due to the West Asia crisis. Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman urged focus on "3Fs"—fuel, fertilizer, and forex—as challenges are externally driven.

articles.headline: US seeking early trade deal, India demands tariff clarity
articles.summary: The US is pushing for an early interim trade deal with India, with a high-power American trade delegation visiting soon. India is cautious, seeking clarity on how US duties will impact its goods and competitors before committing to a pact.

articles.headline: Petrol, diesel prices hiked by over ₹2.50/litre, fourth rise in 10 days
articles.summary: Oil marketing companies on Monday raised retail diesel prices by ₹2.71/litre and petrol by ₹2.61/litre, marking the fourth hike in 10 days. Petrol now costs ₹102.12/litre in Delhi, and diesel ₹95.20/litre.

articles.headline: Economy faces external challenges from high fuel, fertilizer, gold prices
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated that challenges to India's economy are externally driven, citing high and fluctuating global crude oil prices, unimaginable increases in fertilizer prices, and high gold prices due to the West Asia conflict, all requiring foreign exchange payments.

articles.headline: SEBI proposes uniform options strike price norm for exchanges
articles.summary: Market regulator SEBI has proposed a uniform framework for introducing and managing strike prices of options contracts across exchanges, including a mechanism for intra-day additions during volatility, to ensure predictability and availability for ease of trading.

articles.headline: Rising fuel prices shift car buyers to e-scooters, hybrids
articles.summary: Inflated petrol prices, exceeding ₹100/litre in Delhi, are reshaping auto buying behavior, pushing consumers towards electric scooters, hybrids, and fuel-efficient vehicles, especially in entry-level segments. Dealers note visible impact on showroom walk-ins and fresh bookings.

articles.headline: Chemplast Sanmar reports ₹45 crore Q4FY26 net loss
articles.summary: Chemplast Sanmar reported a consolidated net loss of ₹45 crore for Q4FY26, primarily due to an exceptional charge of ₹150 crore related to onerous procurement arrangements from the West Asia crisis. Revenue grew 9% to ₹1,256 crore, and EBITDA margin improved.

articles.headline: Fredun Pharmaceuticals Q4 profit jumps 56% to ₹11 crore
articles.summary: Fredun Pharmaceuticals reported a 56% growth in net profit for Q4 FY26 to ₹11 crore on strong demand. Income increased 27% to ₹213 crore. The company declared a dividend of ₹0.70/share.

articles.headline: Hitachi Energy India Q4 net profit rises 80% to ₹330.5 crore
articles.summary: Hitachi Energy India posted an 80% rise in net profit to ₹330.5 crore during Q4FY26, driven by higher revenues. Revenue rose 46.2%. The board approved a final dividend of ₹8 per share for FY26.

articles.headline: CPCL reworks ₹45,000 cr Nagapattinam project to petrochemical complex
articles.summary: Chennai Petroleum Corporation (CPCL) has re-envisioned its ₹45,000 crore Nagapattinam project as a petrochemical complex, rather than a conventional refinery. It will be a JV with IOC holding 75% and CPCL 25%, aiming to cater to growing petrochemical demand.

articles.headline: Ashok Leyland, VRL Logistics partner for 715 new vehicles
articles.summary: Ashok Leyland has secured an order for 715 vehicles from VRL Logistics, including AVTR 3120 haulage trucks, Boss 1615 trucks, and Oyster staff buses. This marks a strategic deepening of their partnership to enhance logistics efficiency.

articles.headline: Suzlon Q4 profit down 5.75% despite 45% revenue surge
articles.summary: Suzlon Energy reported a 5.75% decline in consolidated net profit to ₹1,114 crore in Q4 FY26, but revenue from operations rose 45% to ₹5,468 crore due to strong execution and robust wind turbine generator business growth. Order-book stands at 5.9 GW.

articles.headline: Hindalco projects FY27 as inflection year for Novelis
articles.summary: Hindalco Industries expects FY27 to be an inflection year for its subsidiary Novelis Inc, with significant capex. India aims for copper self-sufficiency in two years. Hindalco plans ₹12,000 crore capex in FY27.

articles.headline: Shriram Properties Q4 net profit up 65% to ₹78.5 crore
articles.summary: Real estate developer Shriram Properties reported a 65% year-on-year increase in net profit for Q4FY26 to ₹78.5 crore. Total revenue grew 55% to ₹662.7 crore, with bookings at ₹663 crore for the quarter.

articles.headline: Toyota urges India to adopt Brazil's customer-first flex-fuel strategy
articles.summary: Toyota Kirloskar Motor advocates for India to adopt Brazil's customer-first economic approach to flex-fuel, emphasizing consumer savings at the pump rather than just regulatory mandates. This is crucial as India seeks alternatives to petrol and diesel amidst rising crude volatility.

articles.headline: TVS Supply Chain Solutions reports Q4 PAT of ₹18 crore
articles.summary: TVS Supply Chain Solutions reported a consolidated net profit of ₹18 crore for Q4 FY26, a turnaround from a ₹4 crore net loss last year. Revenue rose 12% to ₹3,032 crore. Global CEO Vikas Chadha to become MD from July 1.

articles.headline: RBI forms expert panel on quantum technology in finance
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has established an eight-member expert committee to explore the potential benefits, risks, and challenges of quantum technology in the financial sector, including areas like risk assessment and macro-economic modeling.

articles.headline: NTPC targets four states for 30 GW nuclear projects
articles.summary: State-owned NTPC has initiated site selection studies in Gujarat, Andhra Pradesh, Odisha, and Madhya Pradesh to set up nuclear projects, aiming for 30 GW capacity as part of the government's 100 GW nuclear energy target by 2047.

articles.headline: IIP to undergo major overhaul, include rare earths, gas, water
articles.summary: The Index of Industrial Production (IIP) is set for a major overhaul, with its new 2022-23 series expanding scope to include rare earths, gas, and water supply. The revamp, to be unveiled June 1, aims for a more realistic economic reflection.

articles.headline: Piped natural gas customers barred from buying LPG cylinders
articles.summary: India has prohibited piped natural gas customers from purchasing LPG cylinders with immediate effect. This follows LPG supply shortages caused by the Strait of Hormuz closure due to the US-Israeli war on Iran.

articles.headline: ONGC partners BP arm to boost western offshore production
articles.summary: State-run ONGC has selected BP Exploration Services India (BPXS) as its technical services provider for western offshore fields (excluding Mumbai High), aiming to increase crude oil production by 10.8% and gas production by 31.5% over 10 years.

articles.headline: RBI tightens norms for re-appointing directors on UCB boards
articles.summary: The RBI has tightened rules for urban co-operative banks (UCBs), stating an individual cannot serve as a director continuously for over 10 years, with a compulsory three-year cooling-off period before re-appointment.

articles.headline: Japan PM unveils $19 billion extra budget for fuel subsidies
articles.summary: Japan's Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi announced a $19 billion extra budget to subsidise fuel costs and tackle cost-of-living pressures, amid rising energy prices from the Iran war and a weak yen. The spending will be deficit-financed but won't affect the bond market.

articles.headline: LIC increases stake in Central Bank of India to 6.06%
articles.summary: Life Insurance Corporation (LIC) has hiked its stake in Central Bank of India to 6.06% from 3.16%, acquiring 2.901% additional shares via market purchase on May 22. LIC shares closed 2.93% higher.

articles.headline: Eicher Motors shares climb 6% after strong Q4 earnings
articles.summary: Shares of Eicher Motors rose sharply on Monday after Royal Enfield reported best-ever quarterly earnings. Brokerages reaffirmed buy calls, citing a planned greenfield facility and a new financing JV with Volvo.

articles.headline: OMC stocks jump as fuel prices rise and crude oil falls
articles.summary: Shares of state-owned oil marketing companies gained on Monday after Brent crude settled near $95/barrel, its lowest in over two weeks, and domestic fuel prices were raised for the fourth time in a fortnight, improving earnings outlook for state-run retailers.

articles.headline: India's 5 listed REITs distribute over ₹8,900 cr to unitholders in FY26
articles.summary: India's five listed Real Estate Investment Trusts (REITs) collectively distributed over ₹8,900 crore to 4.25 lakh unitholders in FY26, reflecting strong operating performance and portfolio expansion. The market value stands at over ₹2.72 lakh crore.

articles.headline: VT Markets expands to over 500 US stocks and ETFs
articles.summary: VT Markets, a multi-asset trading platform, has added 39 US stocks and ETFs to its offerings, totaling over 500 instruments. This expansion provides global exposure to high-growth sectors and macroeconomic trends.

articles.headline: Elias George appointed part-time Chairman of Federal Bank
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has approved the appointment of Elias George as the part-time Chairman of Federal Bank for three years. George has served as an independent director since September 2023, with a distinguished career in the IAS.

articles.headline: No blanket extension for ALMM on solar PV cells beyond June 1
articles.summary: The Ministry of New & Renewable Energy (MNRE) announced no blanket extension for the Approved List of Models and Manufacturers (ALMM) List-II deadline for solar PV cells beyond June 1, 2026, after stakeholder consultations and review of war impact advice.

articles.headline: Shriram General, Piramal Finance partner for insurance access
articles.summary: Shriram General Insurance has partnered with Piramal Finance to improve access to insurance solutions. SGI will leverage Piramal Finance’s 701-branch network to offer its insurance products.

articles.headline: Sundaram Finance diversifies as FY26 consolidated net rises 10%
articles.summary: Sundaram Finance is focusing on diversification into secured products like gold loans, used vehicle financing, and MSME lending, alongside geographic expansion. Its consolidated net profit for FY26 grew 10% to ₹2,059 crore, with disbursements reaching ₹32,321 crore.

articles.headline: Government expands Ebola vigil, screens all incoming passengers
articles.summary: The government is intensifying surveillance against the Ebola virus, screening all incoming passengers for symptoms. Instructions were issued to airlines to distribute self-declaration forms for passengers visiting or transiting through Uganda and Democratic Republic of Congo.

articles.headline: Pine Labs posts ₹113 crore FY26 profit, eyes AI and global expansion
articles.summary: Fintech major Pine Labs reported its first full year of profitability with a PAT of ₹113 crore in FY26, a turnaround from a ₹145 crore loss. Revenue grew 19% to ₹2,711 crore. The company is deepening its AI-led push and global expansion.

articles.headline: States urged to cut fuel taxes for consumer relief
articles.summary: Retail fuel prices have risen again, with petrol and diesel increasing by over ₹2.50/litre for the fourth time in 10 days. While the Centre cut excise duty, States haven't, leaving high prices. States like Telangana, AP, Rajasthan have high VAT rates, allowing room for cuts to relieve consumers.

articles.headline: WorkEZ to expand managed office space capacity in South India
articles.summary: Chennai-based WorkEZ plans to expand its managed office space capacity to nearly 35,000 seats in South India over two years. Demand is rising from Global Capability Centers (GCCs), IT firms, and hybrid-work occupiers. Chennai expansion includes new facilities at Phoenix and SSPDL.

articles.headline: Drone logistics start-up Skye Air targets US entry by 2028
articles.summary: Indian drone logistics start-up Skye Air Mobility aims to enter the US market by 2028, planning regulatory trials with the FAA. All drones for the US market will be manufactured in India, initially focusing on healthcare deliveries before expanding to e-commerce.

articles.headline: LTM partners SSP Group to modernize IT infra with AI
articles.summary: LTM has partnered with UK-based SSP Group, a leading food and beverage outlet operator, to deliver modernised, AI-led IT infrastructure support and enhanced application maintenance services. This collaboration aims to boost efficiency and agility using AI automation.

articles.headline: Austerity measures to curb diesel, petrol demand growth post West Asia conflict
articles.summary: Government austerity measures, aimed at economic stability and energy security amid the West Asia conflict, are expected to slow India's transportation fuel demand growth. Kpler revised down India's 2026 refined products demand growth due to higher costs and weaker mobility.

articles.headline: Dell Technologies predicts quantum computing to scale by 2028-29
articles.summary: Dell Technologies predicts quantum computing will scale meaningfully by 2028-29, becoming a major disrupter. Dell CEO Michael Dell confirmed the company's intent to lead in PQC (post quantum cryptography) compliance, calling quantum computing "an additive market".

articles.headline: Cyient Semiconductors raises $30 million at $500 million valuation
articles.summary: Cyient Semiconductors, an arm of Cyient Ltd, has raised $30 million at a $500 million valuation from Edelweiss India Alternatives and co-investors. The funding includes $10 million in equity and $20 million in structured debt for long-duration growth.

articles.headline: Schneider Electric forecasts India data centre boom
articles.summary: Schneider Electric expects its India data centre business to outpace broader operations over the next 4-5 years, driven by surging demand for AI-ready infrastructure. Data centers currently account for 15-20% of its India business and are growing double-digit.

articles.headline: AI smart glasses gain global momentum, but slow India adoption foreseen
articles.summary: Smart AI eyewear is gaining global momentum, with Google unveiling Android XR-powered AI glasses. However, India's adoption is expected to be gradual due to high prices. Experts believe early adoption will be for practical applications like calls and AI assistance.

articles.headline: Rossell Techsys adds US semiconductor client, eyes strong growth
articles.summary: Rossell Techsys is diversifying beyond aerospace and defence, onboarding a major US semiconductor equipment customer and preparing for rapid growth. Revenue rose 62% in Q4 FY26. Semiconductors and space are expected to account for a larger share of future revenue.

articles.headline: Agri gold loans surge 40% as farmers seek faster credit
articles.summary: Agriculture gold loans are growing over 40% as more farmers opt for them, leveraging the surge in gold prices for faster credit. The RBI's clarity on norms and digitalization has eased the process, with average ticket sizes increasing from ₹50,000 to ₹2-2.5 lakh.

articles.headline: South-West monsoon weakens before reaching Kerala coast
articles.summary: The South-West monsoon appears to be slipping on its way to Kerala, with its advance into Sri Lanka and Central Myanmar now expected to lose progress. Fairly strong monsoon-friendly winds over the Arabian Sea suggest a delay in arrival to Kerala.

articles.headline: Mixed trend in rabi crop prices; some above, some below MSP
articles.summary: Rabi crop prices show a mixed trend. Wheat and chana prices were 4-5% below MSP, while masur, mustard, and barley were up to 14% higher than benchmark rates. Maize prices were down 21%.

articles.headline: mooMark, Akiba Farm partner for premium gelato venture in India
articles.summary: mooMark Pvt Ltd has partnered with Japan’s Akiba Farm Holdings to develop a premium gelato business in India. The collaboration combines mooMark’s traceable dairy supply chain with Akiba’s expertise in artisanal dairy production, starting with an innovation mini-lab.

articles.headline: Gold ETF investors exit again as prices drop below $4,600
articles.summary: Investments in physically-backed gold ETFs turned negative again last week, with US and Chinese investors leading outflows over $600 million each. Gold prices dropped below $4,600 an ounce, losing nearly 3% in the month.

articles.headline: Good Food Institute advocates focus on underutilised crops for smart proteins
articles.summary: The Good Food Institute (GFI) India advocates focusing on a wider basket of indigenous and underutilised crops like horse gram, winged bean, and millets for smart protein applications. These crops are nutritious, resilient, and can reduce reliance on a narrow basket of staples.

articles.headline: Global crude steel output falls 1.9% in April; India shows growth
articles.summary: Global crude steel production fell 1.9% year-on-year in April to 153.4 million tonnes, driven by declines in China, the Middle East, and Russia. India, however, posted a 3.9% growth, consolidating its position as the world’s second-largest steel producer.

articles.headline: Centre concerned over State curbs on subsidised fertilizer retailers
articles.summary: The Centre is concerned over States banning sales of non-subsidised fertilizers by subsidised fertilizer retailers, fearing it may backfire by reducing availability of non-chemical nutrient inputs. States aim to stop "tagging" of premium products with subsidised ones.

articles.headline: Centre allocates ₹9,968 crore to Telangana for rural infrastructure
articles.summary: Telangana has been allocated ₹9,968 crore from the 16th Finance Commission grant for developing infrastructure in local bodies, including panchayats and zilla parishads. Guidelines issued specify funds for drinking water, sanitation, streetlights, and rural roads.

articles.headline: PE, VC investments in India halve to $2.7 billion in April
articles.summary: Private equity and venture capital (PE/VC) investments in India fell 50% year-on-year and sequentially to $2.7 billion across 97 deals in April 2026. A slowdown in large-ticket transactions impacted investor activity, though infrastructure and real estate investments saw some large deals.

articles.headline: IIT-Madras unveils new maritime research facility
articles.summary: IIT-Madras launched the ‘Circulating Water Tunnel Facility’, an advanced hydrodynamic testing facility, funded by Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Ltd. The facility was inaugurated at IIT-Madras’ satellite campus in Thaiyur.

articles.headline: Kashmir's first cherry parcel train departs for Mumbai
articles.summary: The season's first cherry parcel train departed from Jammu railway station for Mumbai, transporting around 12 tonnes of cherries. This offers Kashmir's growers a faster route to markets, critical for the perishable fruit which accounts for over 90% of India's production.

articles.headline: AP government to offer ₹1 crore loans to entrepreneurs
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh CM N Chandrababu Naidu announced loans up to ₹1 crore for entrepreneurs to promote MSMEs. He laid the foundation stone for 38 MSME Parks and revealed plans to develop Visakhapatnam as a global data centre hub and Rayalaseema as a defence corridor.

articles.headline: Karnataka CM Siddaramaiah to meet Congress high command in Delhi
articles.summary: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah will meet senior Congress leaders, including Rahul Gandhi and Mallikarjun Kharge, in New Delhi today. Discussions are expected to focus on the long-pending Cabinet reshuffle in Karnataka amid an alleged power tussle.

articles.headline: Cashfree Payments achieves EBITDA profitability, ₹1,000 cr FY26 revenue
articles.summary: Cashfree Payments reported nearly ₹1,000 crore in revenue for FY26 and achieved EBITDA profitability in March 2026. The fintech firm saw 78% q-o-q GTV growth in Q4 and plans a fresh funding round for innovation and global expansion.

articles.headline: TenderCuts plans retail store expansion to boost growth and online sales
articles.summary: Chennai-based omnichannel meat and seafood brand TenderCuts plans to expand its physical retail stores from 18 to 50 in the next 12-18 months, focusing on Chennai. The goal is for physical stores to drive customer acquisition and online sales, aiming for 100% growth in the current fiscal.

articles.headline: West Asia crisis boosts Cochin Port’s bunkering business
articles.summary: The ongoing West Asia crisis and disruption in the Strait of Hormuz are creating new business opportunities for Cochin Port in the bunkering segment. Bunker fuel supply rose to 4.63 lakh tonnes in FY26, as shipping companies seek alternative refuelling locations.

articles.headline: Data protection gaps leave grey areas for AI use of human-generated data
articles.summary: India’s evolving data protection framework contains legal and policy loopholes regarding the use of human-generated data for AI training, raising concerns over consent, transparency, and privacy, especially with the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, yet to be enforced.

articles.headline: Pronto’s use of wearable cameras in home services sparks privacy concerns
articles.summary: Instant home-services start-up Pronto's use of wearable cameras by workers for AI-linked recordings has raised privacy issues. This issue has drawn MeitY's attention, as recordings inside private homes for AI training fall into a "grey area" due to evolving data protection laws.

articles.headline: Air India Express damages runway lights in Muscat
articles.summary: An Air India Express Boeing 737 damaged runway edge lights during takeoff at Muscat, forcing it to stop. The Kannur-bound flight has been grounded for 10 days, and the Oman Transport Safety Bureau is investigating the incident.

articles.headline: ICMR signs 41 public health tech transfer deals with industry
articles.summary: The Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR) has signed 41 technology transfer deals with industry partners for public health technologies including vaccines, diagnostics, and biomedical solutions. This includes transfers for typhoid, Japanese Encephalitis, and Kyasanur Forest Disease.

articles.headline: Torrent Pharma plans India launch of liver drug ‘resmetirom’
articles.summary: Torrent Pharmaceuticals is conducting a phase-3 clinical trial in India for 'resmetirom', a liver disease drug approved in the US for MASH. The company aims to be an early entrant in India's market, where no innovative treatment currently exists for this condition.

articles.headline: Uday Kotak conferred Padma Bhushan by President Murmu
articles.summary: Uday Suresh Kumar Kotak, founder of Kotak Mahindra Bank, was conferred the Padma Bhushan by President Droupadi Murmu during the civil investiture ceremony at Rashtrapati Bhavan in New Delhi on Monday.

articles.headline: Corporate India reshapes B-School hiring, variable pay surges to 40%
articles.summary: Corporate India is shifting B-School hiring priorities towards specialists in supply-chain analytics and AI to manage disruptions. Variable pay, previously 15-20% of compensation, has surged to 40% in some sectors, linking payouts to measurable outcomes as companies prioritize execution and business resilience.

articles.headline: Zydus Lifesciences claims largest Indian oncology player title
articles.summary: Zydus Lifesciences states it is India's largest oncology player, built on a rapidly expanding portfolio of biologics and oral cancer therapies. It is aggressively expanding into US specialty cancer care, leveraging its Indian scale and generics footprint.

articles.headline: India-America trade deal soon: US Foreign Minister Rubio
articles.summary: US Foreign Minister Marco Rubio expressed optimism for a swift India-US trade agreement, expecting finalization in weeks. He reiterated the US's eagerness to host PM Modi for the G20 summit in Florida in December, potentially including a bilateral meeting with President Trump. Rubio also emphasized strong strategic ties and Quad's role in security and critical minerals.

articles.headline: Hyderabad Metro secures ₹13,527 crore refinancing from IRFC
articles.summary: Indian Railway Finance Corporation (IRFC) finalized a ₹13,527 crore loan agreement with Metro Rail (Hyderabad) Limited for refinancing its project debts. This move, following L&T's exit and an agreement with the Telangana government, aims to reduce borrowing costs by about 40%, potentially making the metro profitable next year from a ₹340 crore loss.

articles.headline: Telecom cyber compliance hurdles to be eased by ministries
articles.summary: The Ministries of Communications and Electronics & IT are consulting on easing cyber security audit and compliance for telecom service providers to simplify business processes. While no major regulatory framework changes are planned, the necessity of dual reporting frameworks by companies to different authorities (CERT-In, DoT) will be reviewed.

articles.headline: Indian industries face rising cost pressure from West Asia conflict
articles.summary: Indian industries, from manufacturing to pharma, are grappling with increased costs due to the West Asia conflict, including higher freight and raw material expenses, shipment delays, and supply chain uncertainties. Companies are diversifying raw material sources (e.g., Tata Steel shifting from West Asia), adjusting product prices, and optimizing operations to mitigate margin pressures and adapt to global instability.

articles.headline: Indian pharma firms boost US investment, but exports decline
articles.summary: Indian pharma companies are increasing investment in North America, focusing on specialty drugs and local distribution. While this boosted portfolio value, traditional generics face price pressure. US exports in FY26 dropped 9.98% to $9.47 billion from FY25, despite growth in new drug approvals. Lupin's US generics business is now its largest revenue contributor.

articles.headline: AU Real Estate to invest ₹1,200 crore in Ghaziabad residential project
articles.summary: AU Real Estate will invest ₹1,200 crore in Ghaziabad, Uttar Pradesh, to develop a residential project, 'Cosmos Corner,' in Sidharth Vihar. The project spans 5.6 acres and will comprise approximately 831 residential units. The company announced this investment on Monday.

articles.headline: Puravankara acquires 14.57 acres of land in Bengaluru for ₹2,300 crore project
articles.summary: Real estate firm Puravankara Limited acquired 14.57 acres in Budigere Village, Mandur, Bengaluru, for a residential project valued at about ₹2,300 crore. Of this, 7.92 acres are under a joint development agreement, and 6.65 acres were purchased by the company.

articles.headline: NBCC's Q4 FY26 net profit rises 37% to ₹241.38 crore
articles.summary: Public sector NBCC Limited reported a 37% rise in consolidated net profit to ₹241.38 crore for Q4 FY26, up from ₹175.91 crore in the same period last year. However, total income for the fourth quarter decreased to ₹4,618.59 crore.

articles.headline: CRISIL upgrades Vodafone Idea credit rating to 'A-' with stable outlook
articles.summary: CRISIL upgraded Vodafone Idea's (V) credit rating to 'A-' with a 'stable' outlook, citing plans to raise ₹35,000 crore from banks. This includes ₹25,000 crore in long-term bank loans and ₹10,000 crore in non-fund-based facilities, backed by strong support from the Aditya Birla Group, signalling potential financial stability.

articles.headline: India to reduce refined copper import reliance in two years
articles.summary: Satish Pai, MD of Hindalco Industries, projects India can reduce reliance on imported refined copper within two years by expanding domestic smelting and recycling. Copper imports surged over 350% from ₹22,856 crore in FY17 to ₹1.03 lakh crore in FY26. Hindalco acquired a copper block in 2023 to secure raw material.

articles.headline: SEBI proposes framework for option strike price management
articles.summary: SEBI proposed a framework to manage option strike prices, ensuring sufficient in-the-money and out-of-the-money contracts. This aims to standardize strike intervals, addressing volatility concerns for hedging. The daily review of strike availability around market prices will apply to equity, currency, and commodity options, promoting a systematic and responsive market.

articles.headline: SEBI plans to restart key commodity derivatives department
articles.summary: SEBI plans to reactivate its Commodity Derivatives Market Regulation Department (CDMRD) by July, after merging it with the Market Regulation Department in 2021. This move, following industry requests and renewed regulatory focus, signals a commitment to developing India's commodity derivatives market and addressing unique challenges distinct from other derivatives.

articles.headline: AI contracts surge, but Indian IT earnings growth remains slow
articles.summary: Despite rapid AI adoption, Indian IT sector revenue growth lags, leading to a 33% decline in IT shares in 2026. Global tech budgets are shifting to AI infrastructure, impacting traditional outsourcing. While AI offers new opportunities in areas like data architects and virtual agents, overall tech spending isn't increasing at the same pace, resulting in slower growth for IT service companies.

articles.headline: Dhoot Transmission files DRHP for IPO with SEBI
articles.summary: Bain Capital-backed Dhoot Transmission, an automotive wiring harness and electrical components maker, filed its draft red herring prospectus (DRHP) with SEBI for an Initial Public Offering (IPO). The offering includes a fresh issue of ₹1,400 crore and an Offer For Sale (OFS) of 1.63 crore shares by Venice and Mangalam Capital.

articles.headline: ICICI Bank shares rise on Sandeep Bakhshi's reappointment
articles.summary: ICICI Bank shares rose 2.11% on BSE after RBI approved Sandeep Bakhshi's reappointment as MD and CEO for two years from October 4, 2026. The bank's board had sanctioned his reappointment in January. Bakhshi has served as MD and CEO since October 15, 2018.

articles.headline: Credit card spending increases 7% annually in April 2026
articles.summary: Credit card spending in India increased 7.06% year-on-year to ₹1.97 lakh crore in April 2026. While March saw higher spending (₹2.19 lakh crore) due to the fiscal year-end, point-of-sale transactions grew 8.76% to ₹73,848 crore in April. HDFC Bank recorded a 12.34% rise in its credit card spending.

articles.headline: India and Canada explore vast energy cooperation opportunities
articles.summary: Amid West Asia tensions, Canada seeks new energy export markets like India. A major uranium producer and holder of vast oil/LNG reserves, Canada is investing $500 billion by 2025 in west coast energy infrastructure for LNG exports. India, a large energy importer, aims for secure and clean energy, fostering deeper cooperation in the sector.

articles.headline: Taxpayers face difficulties and delays in GST refunds
articles.summary: Many large taxpayers are experiencing delays in GST refunds, especially for inverted duty structures, despite government directives for provisional refunds within seven days for low-risk cases. Experts and officials note inconsistencies across states in issuing refunds or communicating deficiencies, causing delays in a process meant to be streamlined and efficient.

articles.headline: The cost of state failure: Accountability gaps in public institutions
articles.summary: This article discusses accountability gaps in India: private entities face clear penalties, while public institutions often do not. It advocates for proportionate accountability, differentiating systemic policy failures from individual misconduct, to promote institutional reforms, enhance public trust, and ensure equitable application of laws.

articles.headline: Eyes on SpaceX IPO: Elon Musk's ambitious space vision
articles.summary: SpaceX's anticipated IPO, potentially valued at $1.75-$2 trillion, could be the largest ever. The prospectus outlines Elon Musk's ambitious vision for space colonization, energy production on celestial bodies, and a million-person Martian colony. Despite a $4.9 billion net loss in FY23 on $18.6 billion revenue, Starlink and rocket segments show rapid growth, contributing to Musk's projected wealth.

articles.headline: Price power in crises: Markets more efficient than state controls
articles.summary: This opinion piece argues that in crises, market prices effectively allocate resources and adapt demand, unlike state controls which create unintended consequences and uncertainty. It cites Europe's energy crisis as an example where price signals prompted conservation. India has learned this lesson, allowing market forces to determine prices, urging policymakers to trust prices and protect vulnerable groups.

articles.headline: Weak mango crop expected to drive up prices
articles.summary: Due to intense heat and pest attacks, Uttar Pradesh's Kakori-Malihabad mango belt is facing a weak crop, resulting in smaller fruit and lower yields. Prices for Dasheri mangoes are expected to increase to ₹50-60/kg from ₹30-40/kg last year. Exporters are also dealing with higher insurance premiums and quality issues, limiting international sales.

articles.headline: UP plans special corridor from Nepal border to Bundelkhand
articles.summary: The UP government is developing a 'North-South Corridor' to connect Nepal border districts to central and southern states via Prayagraj, Chitrakoot, and Bundelkhand. This project aims to boost industrial, agricultural, and tourism sectors, improving connectivity and market access. The first 220 km corridor, Kushinagar-Deoria-Noida-Ghazipur-Jamaniya, is under construction.

articles.headline: India to export sugar to US despite domestic ban
articles.summary: India will export 8,606 tonnes of raw cane sugar to the US under the tariff-rate quota (TRQ) scheme for October 2025-September 2026. This export is permitted despite India's general ban on sugar exports until December 30, implemented to ensure domestic supply and stabilize prices amidst lower production and increased ethanol diversion.

articles.headline: Domestic flight operations to decrease by 7% in June
articles.summary: Domestic flights in India are forecast to drop by 7% in June, with 20,670 flights compared to 22,220 last year. Hyderabad airport (GMR) will see the largest cut with 371 fewer weekly flights. Rising Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF) costs (40% of operational expenses) and a stronger dollar are impacting airlines, with some carriers like full-service carrier operating 28% fewer flights.

articles.headline: Increasing AC efficiency can save ₹2.5 lakh crore, prevent energy crisis
articles.summary: A UC Berkeley study reveals that doubling AC energy efficiency could save Indian consumers ₹2.5 lakh crore ($25 billion) over a decade and alleviate energy shortages. With 13-15 crore new ACs expected by 2030, electricity demand could surge 120-180 GW, risking blackouts and higher costs without improved policy.

articles.headline: Petrol crosses ₹100 in Delhi after fourth price hike
articles.summary: State-run oil companies raised petrol and diesel prices for the fourth time in two weeks, taking petrol in Delhi past the ₹100-mark for the first time since 2022. Petrol rose by ₹2.61 per litre to ₹102.12, while diesel increased by ₹2.71 to ₹95.20. Despite a slight dip in global crude prices, companies are recouping past losses and under-recoveries on auto fuels and cooking gas.

articles.headline: Quad foreign ministers to meet in Delhi over trade and energy
articles.summary: Foreign ministers from India, Australia, Japan, and the US will meet in New Delhi on Tuesday for the first Quad meeting in a year. S Jaishankar, Penny Wong, Toshimitsu Motegi, and Marco Rubio will discuss maritime security, energy supplies, and critical minerals. The meeting comes amid global trade uncertainty triggered by the West Asia conflict.

articles.headline: Supreme Court slams NTA, Centre over recurring NEET-UG paper leaks
articles.summary: The Supreme Court issued notices to the Centre and NTA regarding the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak, expressing disappointment that lessons were not learned from past incidents. A bench led by Justice PS Narasimha sought details on the Expert Committee’s recommendations and monitoring steps. The petitions argue for replacing NTA with a more accountable statutory body.

articles.headline: Markets recover as crude eases on Gulf peace hopes
articles.summary: Indian markets staged a sharp recovery on Monday, driven by easing global risk appetite after US President Donald Trump indicated a peace deal with Iran was "largely negotiated," pulling down crude prices. The Nifty 50 closed above 24,000.

articles.headline: SEBI approves Executive Director appointments for CDSL
articles.summary: Central Depository Services (India) Limited (CDSL) received SEBI's approval for the appointments of Amit Mahajan and Nayana Ovalekar as Executive Directors. Mahajan will manage key operations, while Ovalekar will handle regulatory, compliance, risk management, and investor grievance matters for the company.

articles.headline: JM Financial launches second private credit fund (SCIF 2)
articles.summary: JM Financial Asset Management introduced its second credit-focused Alternative Investment Fund (AIF), JM Financial Select Credit Fund-2 (SCIF 2), a Category II AIF with a ₹1,000 crore base corpus and a greenshoe option. This initiative targets India's growing private credit sector as companies seek diverse funding sources.

articles.headline: Copper prices rise on US-Iran peace deal hopes
articles.summary: Three-month copper on the LME rose 0.9% to $13,635/tonne, and Shanghai Futures Exchange gained 1.1% to 105,650 yuan. Prices rose due to hopes of a US-Iran peace deal, easing inflation and global economic slowdown fears.

articles.headline: External shocks pressure rupee, reserves; crude oil softens: FM Sitharaman
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated India's forex reserves are pressured by high crude oil, fertilizer, and gold prices due to global instability, particularly in West Asia, though the economy remains robust. Crude oil prices fell 5.7% to $94.6/barrel, boosting the rupee by 0.5% to 95.23/dollar, and propelling Sensex up 1.4%. She urged SIDBI to create special loan models for MSMEs, ensuring PSUs clear dues within 45 days.
